name: Regeneration
description: Terra Nil reimagines city-building as world-healing. Across games, films and animation, creators explore renewal instead of conquest – asking how we might restore balance, rebuild damaged ecosystems, and live alongside nature rather than above it.

key_work.title: Terra Nil
key_work.brief_description:
Terra Nil (2023) is an antidote to city builder and strategy games.

While many city-builder games focus on exploiting environmental resources, colonising land and unstainable expansion, Terra Nil asks players to bring a wasteland back to life by restoring nature and building healthy ecosystems.

The game asks: ‘What if we put nature first?’

The creators, Free Lives, were inspired by their home in Cape Town, South Africa. They based parts of the game on local scrublands, which are small but important ecosystems, which are under constant threat. They also looked at real conservation efforts happening nearby.

Terra Nil uses real ideas from environmental science, like rewilding and controlled burning to fight climate change. The game encourages players to consider new possibilities for a sustainable future. A percentage of the games profit is donated to a non-profit wildlife organisation.

end.title: WALL·E
end.brief_description: WALL·E is the last robot left on an Earth that has been overrun with garbage and all humans have fled to outer space. For 700 years he has continued to try and clean up the mess, but has developed some rather interesting human-like qualities.

constellations_other.name: Plants and people
constellations_other.description: Plants can be characters in stories, and we often project human qualities onto them, but how do they see the world?

constellations_other.name: Distributed Consciousness
constellations_other.description: Distributed Consciousness mixes NFT technology and custom AI software created by the artist to question artificial intelligence, ecology, sustainability, consciousness and free will - just like the works in this Constellation.

constellations_other.name: Regeneration
constellations_other.description: Terra Nil reimagines city-building as world-healing. Across games, films and animation, creators explore renewal instead of conquest – asking how we might restore balance, rebuild damaged ecosystems, and live alongside nature rather than above it.

constellations_other.name: Silent adventures (Hollow Knight - Education)
constellations_other.description: Videogames, film and TV usually rely on the spoken word to communicate character and story. It wasn’t always this way. Although we may think of ‘silent films’ as in the past, there are contemporary videogames and films that shy away from using the spoken word.

links.description: Both works start in worlds devastated by human neglect. WALL·E’s lonely robot and Terra Nil’s restoration drones share quiet optimism, showing that healing begins with small acts of care, patience, and attention, turning ruin into spaces of wonder.

end.title: Journey
end.brief_description: A third-person adventure game in which the player, controlling a robed figure, makes a pilgrimage through a desert landscape to a rugged mountain with a beacon of light in the distance while uncovering the history of their people, rescuing and cooperating with friendly creatures, avoiding predatory ones and communicating with other travelers.

constellations_other.name: Regeneration
constellations_other.description: Terra Nil reimagines city-building as world-healing. Across games, films and animation, creators explore renewal instead of conquest – asking how we might restore balance, rebuild damaged ecosystems, and live alongside nature rather than above it.

links.description: Like WALL·E, Journey places players in barren landscapes and invites reflection through exploration. Both replace words with atmosphere, rhythm and interaction, offering a meditative path of renewal where connection, discovery and persistence are the tools for restoring beauty.

end.title: Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind
end.creator_credit: Hayao Miyazaki, Naoki Kaneko, Shouji Sakai, Tomoko Kida, Isao Takahata, Toshio Suzuki and Topcraft

video_links.name: Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind - Official Trailer

end.brief_description: After a global war, the seaside kingdom known as the Valley of the Wind remains one of the last strongholds on Earth untouched by a poisonous jungle and the powerful insects that guard it. Led by the courageous Princess Nausicaä, the people of the Valley engage in an epic struggle to restore the bond between humanity and Earth.

constellations_other.description: Terra Nil reimagines city-building as world-healing. Across games, films and animation, creators explore renewal instead of conquest – asking how we might restore balance, rebuild damaged ecosystems, and live alongside nature rather than above it.

constellations_other.name: The forest for the trees
constellations_other.description: Marshmallow Laser Feast's Sanctuary of the Unseen Forest celebrates the majestic Colombian kapok tree. Let's look back at some of screen culture's other mighty trees and forests.

constellations_other.name: Change through actions
constellations_other.description: Young people are increasingly being seen on screen as the faces of environmental advocacy and care. Explore how these films, TV series, and videogames explore environmental issues and solutions from the perspectives of young people.

links.description: Hayao Miyazaki’s Nausicaä imagines a toxic world recovering through empathy and understanding. Like Journey, it values quiet connection over conquest, suggesting that progress comes from care, curiosity and balance rather than domination.

end.title: Flower
end.brief_description:
ESCAPE TO A DREAMLIKE WORLD

From the studio behind the genre-bending titles Journey and flOw comes Flower, a unique gaming experience that lets you soar through a dreamlike world of luscious fields and rolling hills. Control the wind to carry a stream of flower petals around each landscape and watch as it comes to life.

Every level has its own unique aims and little mysteries – but you don’t have to worry about them if you don’t feel like it. Flower is about doing what you want, when you want. Freedom is everything.

constellations_other.name: Regeneration
constellations_other.description: Terra Nil reimagines city-building as world-healing. Across games, films and animation, creators explore renewal instead of conquest – asking how we might restore balance, rebuild damaged ecosystems, and live alongside nature rather than above it.

links.description: In Flower, players guide petals across empty fields, bringing colour and life back to the landscape. Like Nausicaä, it focuses on ecological renewal, showing that healing the world can be deeply emotional.

end.title: Avatar 2
end.creator_credit: James Cameron, Deborah Lynn Scott, Jon Landau, 20th Century Studios, Lightstorm Entertainment, River Road Entertainment, TSG Entertainment, Ben Procter, Dylan Cole, Josh Friedman, Brian Adler, Daniel McGraw, Joe Howes and Shane Salerno
end.brief_description: Twelve years after exploring Pandora and joining the Na’vi, Jake Sully has since raised a family with Neytiri and established himself within the clans of the new world. Of course, peace can only last so long. Especially when the military organization from the original film returns to “finish what they started”.

constellations_other.name: Go Bang
constellations_other.description: Australian dance act PNAU are renowned for groundbreaking visuals in their music videos, including for their 2017 single 'Go Bang' which featured Kira Divine.

constellations_other.name: Regeneration
constellations_other.description: Terra Nil reimagines city-building as world-healing. Across games, films and animation, creators explore renewal instead of conquest – asking how we might restore balance, rebuild damaged ecosystems, and live alongside nature rather than above it.

links.description: Both works celebrate symbiotic relationships between humans and the natural world. Avatar’s glowing oceans echo Flower’s flowing colour trails, turning environmental awareness into spectacle and inviting viewers to feel the pulse of living ecosystems through movement, light and sound.

end.title: Planet Earth II

video_links.name: Planet Earth II: Trailer - BBC One
Planet Earth II: Official Extended Trailer | BBC Earth
Blue Planet II Official Trailer 2 | BBC Earth

end.brief_description: David Attenborough presents a documentary series exploring how animals meet the challenges of surviving in the most iconic habitats on earth.

constellations_other.name: Regeneration
constellations_other.description: Terra Nil reimagines city-building as world-healing. Across games, films and animation, creators explore renewal instead of conquest – asking how we might restore balance, rebuild damaged ecosystems, and live alongside nature rather than above it.

links.description: Planet Earth II transforms real ecosystems into cinematic experiences. Like Avatar, it reveals how sound and image can evoke empathy for non-human life, showing that immersive visual storytelling can inspire awe and care for fragile, interconnected environments.
